title: Debug an implicit/given resolution
description: Find which instance the compiler selected and why, then fix ambiguity or a missing instance.
body: Steps: 1. Reproduce the error (ambiguous implicit, could not find implicit, or wrong behavior). 2. Use the compiler's implicit-search trace (-Vimplicits / -Xlog-implicits) or summon[T]/implicitly[T] to see what resolves. 3. Identify the candidate scopes: local, imported, and companion objects. 4. For ambiguity, remove a competing import or move the canonical instance to the companion; for missing, provide the instance in scope. 5. Recompile and confirm the intended instance is chosen. Rollback point: before reorganizing instance locations. Validation: a clean compile with no ambiguity and a test asserting the selected instance's behavior (e.g. the right Ordering).
